payload.description: CHAR_A: "It's wrong. The whole thing is wrong."

cast.loves: bringing an idea into the world

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It's wrong. The whole thing is wrong.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You've said that six times.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Because it's been wrong six times.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Okay but — wrong how.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Look at this part. It should connect here. It doesn't.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Has it ever connected?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: No. That's kind of the whole thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...Yeah. Okay. That's bad.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Can I see it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You won't see the problem.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Rude. Also you still handed it over.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I'm watching your face. Your face confirms it's bad.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The door was open.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: The door is never open.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: It was open. I brought snacks. What's happening in here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Nothing. A crisis. Same thing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: How long has it been like this.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Since before I got here.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: But it's going to be okay, right.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...I mean. Define okay.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: What if you started from the part that works.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Oh no. That's a new silence.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: That is. Actually. A different approach.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Tell me what to hold.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: This part. Don't move.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I'm just here spiritually. Also emotionally. Also I've got snacks.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Bloom. You're carrying the team right now, fr.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It connected.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I know. I felt it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: SEE. I said it would be okay.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You said that after it was already okay.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: That's called believing in people.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s love of bringing an idea into the world and fear of the thing not being good drive the sequence's ignition — the crisis is entirely internal, a failed connection on something they built, and no external enemy is needed. They hand over `<PROP_A>` before they finish protesting, because some part of them needs a second pair of eyes even when they resist it. `<CHAR_B>`'s love of being understood and fear of being overlooked surfaces not in complaint but in action — they keep asking the right questions, hold things literally, and the turning point beat ("I know. I felt it") is the moment they get to be seen doing something that mattered. `<CHAR_C>`'s love of believing in people and fear of being made to feel naive is the engine of the breakthrough — they offer the reframe without irony, and the closing beat plays their earnestness straight rather than as a joke. The home location shapes everything: the door was already open, there's ambient clutter, and the stakes feel urgent precisely because they're playing out somewhere ordinary and familiar. No one is performing for an audience; the pressure is entirely internal, which is what makes the quiet resolution land.
